sentences.sentence: When asked 'Should robots be used to colonise other planets?', cosmology and astrophysics Professor Martin Rees said he believed the solar system would be mapped by robotic craft by the end of the century.
sentences.translation: 当被问到“机器人是否应被用来殖民其他星球？”时，宇宙学与天体物理学教授马丁·里斯说，他认为到本世纪末，太阳系将由机器人飞船完成绘图。

structure.text: When asked 'Should robots be used to colonise other planets?'
cosmology and astrophysics Professor Martin Rees
he believed the solar system would be mapped by robotic craft by the end of the century
would be mapped by robotic craft

difficult_points: When asked 是省略主语和 be 动词的非谓语结构，表示“当他被问到……时”。
he believed 后省略 that，引出宾语从句说明里斯的看法。
would be mapped 是过去将来时的被动语态，表示从 said 的时间点看未来会发生的事。

expressions.text: by the end of the century

expressions.meaning_zh: 机器人飞船；无人航天器

sentences.sentence: The next step would be mining asteroids, enabling fabrication of large structures in space without having to bring all the raw materials from Earth.
sentences.translation: 下一步将是开采小行星，从而无需从地球带来全部原材料，就能在太空中制造大型结构。

structure.text: enabling fabrication of large structures in space
without having to bring all the raw materials from Earth

difficult_points: mining asteroids 是动名词短语作表语，说明“下一步”的具体内容。
enabling... 是现在分词短语，表示前面“开采小行星”带来的结果。
without having to bring... 用介词 without 加动名词，表达“不必……”。

sentences.sentence: ... I think this is more realistic and benign than the ... "terraforming"* of planets.'
sentences.translation: ……我认为这比对行星进行所谓“地球化改造”更加现实，也更温和。

structure.text: this is more realistic and benign than the ... "terraforming"* of planets
more realistic and benign than the ... "terraforming"* of planets

difficult_points: 句首省略号表示引文中间有删节，不影响主句 I think 的判断结构。
this 指前一句所说的开采小行星并在太空制造结构这一方案。
more... than... 构成比较结构，将该方案与行星“地球化改造”进行对比。

sentences.sentence: He maintains that colonised planets 'should be preserved with a status that is analogous to Antarctica here on Earth.'
sentences.translation: 他坚持认为，被殖民的星球“应当以类似地球上南极洲的地位被保留下来”。

structure.text: that colonised planets 'should be preserved with a status that is analogous to Antarctica here on Earth.'
should be preserved with a status
that is analogous to Antarctica here on Earth

difficult_points: maintains 后接 that 引导的宾语从句，表达里斯坚持的观点。
colonised planets 是过去分词作前置定语，表示“被殖民的星球”。
that is analogous to... 是定语从句，修饰 status，说明这种地位类似南极洲。

sentences.sentence: On the question of using robots to colonise other planets and exploit mineral resources, engineering Professor Daniel Wolpert replied, 'I don't see a pressing need to colonise other planets unless we can bring [these] resources back to Earth.
sentences.translation: 关于利用机器人殖民其他星球并开发矿产资源的问题，工程学教授丹尼尔·沃尔珀特回答说：“除非我们能把这些资源带回地球，否则我看不出殖民其他星球有什么迫切需要。

structure.text: On the question of using robots to colonise other planets and exploit mineral resources
I don't see a pressing need to colonise other planets unless we can bring [these] resources back to Earth
unless we can bring [these] resources back to Earth

difficult_points: On the question of... 是介词短语放在句首，限定回答所针对的问题。
using robots to colonise... and exploit... 中两个不定式并列，说明使用机器人的目的。
unless 引导条件状语从句，表示“除非能把资源带回地球”。

sentences.sentence: The vast majority of Earth is currently inaccessible to us.
sentences.translation: 地球上的绝大部分区域目前对我们来说仍然无法到达。
difficult_points: The vast majority of... 作主语，强调范围是“绝大部分”。
inaccessible to us 表示“对我们而言无法进入或到达”，不是“不可理解”。

sentences.sentence: Using robots to gather resources nearer to home would seem to be a better use of our robotic tools.'
sentences.translation: 用机器人去收集离地球更近的资源，似乎会是对机器人工具更好的利用。”

structure.text: Using robots to gather resources nearer to home
a better use of our robotic tools
to gather resources nearer to home

difficult_points: Using robots... 是动名词短语作整句主语。
nearer to home 指相对于其他星球而言更接近地球的位置。
would seem to be 语气较委婉，表示“似乎会是”。

sentences.sentence: Meanwhile, for anthropology Professor Kathleen Richardson, the idea of 'colonisation' of other planets seemed morally dubious: 'I think whether we do something on Earth or on Mars we should always do it in the spirit of a genuine interest in "the Other", not to impose a particular model, but to meet "the Other".'
sentences.translation: 与此同时，对人类学教授凯瑟琳·理查森来说，殖民其他星球这一想法在道德上似乎可疑：“我认为，无论我们是在地球上还是在火星上做什么，都应始终本着对‘他者’真正感兴趣的精神去做，不是为了强加某种特定模式，而是为了与‘他者’相遇。”

structure.text: the idea of 'colonisation' of other planets
I think whether we do something on Earth or on Mars we should always do it in the spirit of a genuine interest in "the Other", not to impose a particular model, but to meet "the Other"
whether we do something on Earth or on Mars

difficult_points: for Kathleen Richardson 表示“在她看来”，不是表示目的。
whether... or... 引导让步含义，表示无论是在地球还是火星。
not to impose..., but to meet... 是并列的不定式结构，形成“不是……而是……”的对比。

sentences.sentence: In response to the second question, 'How soon will machine intelligence outstrip human intelligence?', Rees mentions robots that are advanced enough to beat humans at chess, but then goes on to say, 'Robots are still limited in their ability to sense their environment: they can't yet recognise and move the pieces on a real chessboard as cleverly as a child can.
sentences.translation: 在回答第二个问题“机器智能多久会超过人类智能？”时，里斯提到有些机器人已经先进到足以在国际象棋中击败人类，但随后又接着说：“机器人在感知环境的能力上仍然有限：它们还不能像孩子那样灵巧地识别并移动真实棋盘上的棋子。

structure.text: In response to the second question
mentions robots that are advanced enough to beat humans at chess
that are advanced enough to beat humans at chess
but then goes on to say
as cleverly as a child can

difficult_points: that are advanced enough... 是定语从句，修饰 robots。
advanced enough to beat... 中 enough 后接不定式，表示“足够先进以至于能……”。
but then goes on to say 表示转折后继续说明，后面的引语削弱了前面对机器人能力的印象。
as cleverly as a child can 是比较结构，can 后省略 recognise and move the pieces。

sentences.sentence: Later this century, however, their more advanced successors may relate to their surroundings, and to people, as adeptly as we do.
sentences.translation: 然而，到本世纪晚些时候，它们更先进的后继者也许能像我们一样熟练地与周围环境以及人打交道。

structure.text: may relate to their surroundings, and to people

difficult_points: Later this century 表示“本世纪晚些时候”，是时间状语。
their more advanced successors 指前文 robots 的后续更高级版本。
as adeptly as we do 中 do 替代 relate to their surroundings, and to people，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: Moral questions then arise.
sentences.translation: 于是，道德问题便随之出现。
difficult_points: then 表示在机器人能力提升之后，逻辑上接着出现的问题。
arise 是不及物动词，表示问题“出现／产生”，不需要宾语。

sentences.sentence: ... Should we feel guilty about exploiting [sophisticated robots]?
sentences.translation: ……我们是否应当因为利用那些高级机器人而感到内疚？
difficult_points: Should we... 是情态动词提前构成的一般疑问句。
feel guilty about 后接动名词 exploiting，表示“因……而内疚”。
方括号中的 sophisticated robots 是编辑补足内容，用来明确代词或省略信息。

sentences.sentence: Should we fret if they are underemployed, frustrated, or bored?'
sentences.translation: 如果它们就业不足、感到沮丧或无聊，我们是否应当为此担忧？”

structure.text: if they are underemployed, frustrated, or bored

difficult_points: Should we fret 与前一句 Should we feel guilty 并列，继续提出道德疑问。
if 引导条件状语从句，说明产生担忧的条件。
underemployed, frustrated, or bored 三个形容词并列作表语，用于拟人化描述机器人状态。

sentences.sentence: Wolpert's response to the question about machine intelligence outstripping human intelligence was this: 'In a limited sense it already has.
sentences.translation: 沃尔珀特对机器智能超过人类智能这一问题的回答是：“从有限意义上说，它已经超过了。

structure.text: Wolpert's response to the question about machine intelligence outstripping human intelligence
In a limited sense it already has

difficult_points: response to the question about... 是主语核心 response 的后置修饰。
outstripping human intelligence 是动名词结构，说明 question 的内容。
it already has 中 has 代替 has outstripped human intelligence，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: Machines can already navigate, remember and search for items with an ability that far outstrips humans.
sentences.translation: 机器已经能够以远超人类的能力导航、记忆并搜索物品。

structure.text: can already navigate, remember and search for items
with an ability that far outstrips humans

difficult_points: navigate, remember and search 三个动词并列，共用情态动词 can。
with an ability... 是方式状语，说明机器完成这些动作时所具备的能力。
that far outstrips humans 是定语从句，修饰 ability，far 用来加强 outstrips 的程度。

sentences.sentence: However, there is no machine that can identify visual objects or speech with the reliability and flexibility of humans.
sentences.translation: 然而，还没有机器能以人类那样的可靠性和灵活性识别视觉物体或语音。

structure.text: that can identify visual objects or speech with the reliability and flexibility of humans
with the reliability and flexibility of humans

difficult_points: However 引出转折，指出机器在某些能力上仍不如人类。
there is no machine 是存在句结构，强调“不存在这样的机器”。
that 引导定语从句，具体限定 no machine 的特征。

sentences.sentence: ... Expecting a machine close to the creative intelligence of a human within the next 50 years would be highly ambitious.'
sentences.translation: ……期待在未来50年内出现一台接近人类创造性智能的机器，将是非常雄心勃勃的想法。”

structure.text: Expecting a machine close to the creative intelligence of a human within the next 50 years
a machine close to the creative intelligence of a human

difficult_points: Expecting... 是动名词短语作主语，整件“期待”的事情被评价为 ambitious。
close to the creative intelligence of a human 后置修饰 a machine。
within the next 50 years 限定时间范围，说明这种期待的时间条件。

expressions.meaning_zh: 非常雄心勃勃；难度很高

sentences.sentence: Richardson believes that our fear of machines becoming too advanced has more to do with human nature than anything intrinsic to the machines themselves.
sentences.translation: 理查森认为，我们对机器变得过于先进的恐惧，与其说源于机器本身的内在特性，不如说更多与人性有关。

structure.text: that our fear of machines becoming too advanced has more to do with human nature than anything intrinsic to the machines themselves
our fear of machines becoming too advanced
has more to do with human nature than anything intrinsic to the machines themselves

difficult_points: that 引导 believes 的宾语从句，是理查森观点的具体内容。
machines becoming too advanced 是动名词复合结构，修饰 fear 的来源。
more to do with... than... 构成比较，强调恐惧主要源自人性而非机器本身。

sentences.sentence: In her view, it stems from humans' tendency to personify inanimate objects: we create machines based on representations of ourselves, imagine that machines think and behave as we do, and therefore see them as an autonomous threat.
sentences.translation: 在她看来，这种恐惧源自人类将无生命物体拟人化的倾向：我们按照自身形象创造机器，想象机器像我们一样思考和行动，因此把它们视为一种自主威胁。

structure.text: stems from humans' tendency to personify inanimate objects
we create machines based on representations of ourselves, imagine that machines think and behave as we do, and therefore see them as an autonomous threat
that machines think and behave as we do

difficult_points: it 指代上一句中的 our fear of machines becoming too advanced。
冒号后的内容解释 humans' tendency to personify inanimate objects 的具体表现。
create, imagine, see 三个谓语动词并列，描述人类如何一步步把机器看成威胁。
as we do 中 do 替代 think and behave。

sentences.sentence: 'One of the consequences of thinking that the problem lies with machines is that ... we tend to imagine they are greater and more powerful than they really are and subsequently they become so.'
sentences.translation: “认为问题出在机器身上的后果之一是……我们往往会想象它们比实际更强大，随后它们也就真的变成那样。”

structure.text: One of the consequences of thinking that the problem lies with machines
thinking that the problem lies with machines
that the problem lies with machines
that ... we tend to imagine they are greater and more powerful than they really are and subsequently they become so
they are greater and more powerful than they really are

difficult_points: One of the consequences... 是主语，谓语 is 后接 that 引导的表语从句。
thinking that... 中 that 引导 thinking 的宾语从句，说明这种想法的内容。
than they really are 是比较从句，指出想象中的能力高于真实情况。
they become so 中 so 指代前面的 greater and more powerful。

sentences.sentence: This led on to the third question, 'Should we be scared by advances in artificial intelligence?'
sentences.translation: 这引出了第三个问题：“我们是否应当害怕人工智能的发展？”

structure.text: Should we be scared by advances in artificial intelligence?

difficult_points: This 指代前文关于机器智能是否超过人类智能的讨论。
led on to 表示讨论自然过渡到下一个问题。
引号中的问句是 the third question 的同位说明。

sentences.sentence: To this question, Rees replied, 'Those who should be worried are the futurologists who believe in the so-called "singularity".**
sentences.translation: 对于这个问题，里斯回答说：“真正应当担心的是那些相信所谓‘奇点’的未来学家。

structure.text: Those who should be worried are the futurologists who believe in the so-called "singularity"

difficult_points: Those who... 是“先行词 + 定语从句”结构，整体作引语中主句的主语。
两个 who 从句分别修饰 Those 和 futurologists，层次不同。
so-called 表示“所谓的”，暗示说话人对 singularity 这一概念保持距离。

sentences.sentence: ... And another worry is that we are increasingly dependent on computer networks, and that these could behave like a single "brain" with a mind of its own, and with goals that may be contrary to human welfare.
sentences.translation: ……另一个令人担忧之处是，我们越来越依赖计算机网络，而这些网络可能会像一个拥有自我意识的单一“大脑”那样行动，并且带有可能违背人类福祉的目标。

structure.text: that we are increasingly dependent on computer networks
that these could behave like a single "brain" with a mind of its own, and with goals that may be contrary to human welfare
that may be contrary to human welfare

difficult_points: is 后并列两个 that 从句，共同说明 another worry 的内容。
these 指代前面的 computer networks。
with a mind of its own 和 with goals... 都修饰 a single “brain” 式的行为状态。
that may be contrary to human welfare 是定语从句，修饰 goals。

expressions.text: with a mind of its own

sentences.sentence: I think we should ensure that robots remain as no more than "idiot savants" lacking the capacity to outwit us, even though they may greatly surpass us in the ability to calculate and process information.'
sentences.translation: 我认为我们应当确保机器人至多只是缺乏智胜我们能力的“白痴学者”，即使它们在计算和处理信息的能力上可能大大超过我们。

structure.text: we should ensure that robots remain as no more than "idiot savants" lacking the capacity to outwit us
that robots remain as no more than "idiot savants" lacking the capacity to outwit us
lacking the capacity to outwit us
even though they may greatly surpass us in the ability to calculate and process information

difficult_points: think 后省略 that，宾语从句中又包含 ensure 后的 that 从句。
no more than 表示“仅仅；至多”，强调机器人能力应被限制。
lacking the capacity to outwit us 是现在分词短语，后置修饰 “idiot savants”。
even though 引导让步状语从句，承认机器人在计算和信息处理方面可能很强。

expressions.text: surpass us in the ability to

sentences.sentence: Wolpert's response was to say that we have already seen the damaging effects of artificial intelligence in the form of computer viruses.
sentences.translation: 沃尔珀特的回应是说，我们已经看到人工智能以计算机病毒的形式产生了破坏性影响。

structure.text: that we have already seen the damaging effects of artificial intelligence in the form of computer viruses
the damaging effects of artificial intelligence
in the form of computer viruses

difficult_points: was to say 用不定式作表语，说明 response 的具体内容。
that 引导 say 的宾语从句，包含现在完成时 have already seen。
in the form of... 表示破坏性影响呈现的具体形式。

sentences.sentence: 'But in this case,' he says, 'the real intelligence is the malicious designer.
sentences.translation: “但在这种情况下，”他说，“真正的智能来自恶意设计者。
difficult_points: he says 插入在直接引语中间，是引述语，不影响引语主干。
the real intelligence is the malicious designer 将真正的“智能”归因于人类设计者而非病毒本身。
malicious designer 指有恶意的人类设计者，承接上一句的 computer viruses。

sentences.sentence: Critically, the benefits of computers outweigh the damage that computer viruses cause.
sentences.translation: 关键在于，计算机带来的好处超过了计算机病毒造成的损害。

structure.text: the damage that computer viruses cause

difficult_points: Critically 是句子副词，强调后面判断是关键点。
outweigh 表示“超过；比……更重要”，用于比较利益和损害。
that computer viruses cause 是定语从句，修饰 damage。

sentences.sentence: Similarly, while there may be misuses of robotics in the near future, the benefits that they will bring are likely to outweigh these negative aspects.'
sentences.translation: 同样，尽管在不久的将来机器人技术可能会被滥用，但它们将带来的好处很可能会超过这些负面方面。”

structure.text: while there may be misuses of robotics in the near future
the benefits that they will bring
are likely to outweigh these negative aspects

difficult_points: while 引导让步状语从句，表示“尽管可能存在滥用”。
there may be 是存在句，表达未来可能出现的情况。
that they will bring 是定语从句，修饰 benefits。
are likely to 表示可能性较高，而不是确定发生。

sentences.sentence: Richardson's response to this question was this: 'We need to ask why fears of artificial intelligence and robots persist; none have in fact risen up and challenged human supremacy.'
sentences.translation: 理查森对这个问题的回应是：“我们需要追问，为什么对人工智能和机器人的恐惧会持续存在；事实上，没有任何机器人曾经起而反抗并挑战人类的至高地位。”

structure.text: We need to ask why fears of artificial intelligence and robots persist
why fears of artificial intelligence and robots persist
none have in fact risen up and challenged human supremacy

difficult_points: why 引导 ask 的宾语从句，提出需要追问的内容。
分号连接两个密切相关的分句，后半句用事实回应前半句的恐惧。
none 指没有任何人工智能或机器人，have risen up and challenged 是现在完成时。

sentences.sentence: She believes that as robots have never shown themselves to be a threat to humans, it seems unlikely that they ever will.
sentences.translation: 她认为，由于机器人从未表现出对人类构成威胁，它们似乎也不太可能在将来构成威胁。

structure.text: that as robots have never shown themselves to be a threat to humans, it seems unlikely that they ever will
as robots have never shown themselves to be a threat to humans
it seems unlikely that they ever will

difficult_points: believes 后接 that 引导的宾语从句，说明她的判断。
as 引导原因状语从句，表示“由于机器人从未……”。
it seems unlikely that... 中 it 是形式主语，真正主语是 that 从句。
they ever will 中 will 后省略 show themselves to be a threat to humans。

sentences.sentence: In fact, she went on, 'Not all fear [robots]; many people welcome machine intelligence.'
sentences.translation: 事实上，她接着说：“并非所有人都害怕机器人；许多人欢迎机器智能。”
difficult_points: went on 表示“继续说”，承接前一句理查森的观点。
Not all 是部分否定，意思是“并非所有人都……”，不是“所有人都不”。
分号连接两个并列分句，后半句进一步说明有许多人欢迎机器智能。

sentences.sentence: In answer to the fourth question, 'What can science fiction tell us about robotics?', Rees replied, 'I sometimes advise students that it's better to read first-rate science fiction than second-rate science – more stimulating, and perhaps no more likely to be wrong.'
sentences.translation: 在回答第四个问题“科幻小说能告诉我们关于机器人学的什么？”时，里斯回答说：“我有时建议学生，与其读二流科学，不如读一流科幻小说——它更有启发性，而且也许并不更可能出错。”

structure.text: In answer to the fourth question
I sometimes advise students that it's better to read first-rate science fiction than second-rate science
that it's better to read first-rate science fiction than second-rate science
more stimulating, and perhaps no more likely to be wrong

difficult_points: In answer to... 是介词短语作状语，说明里斯是在回答第四个问题。
advise students that... 中 that 引导宾语从句，说明建议内容。
better to read... than... 是比较结构，比较两种阅读对象。
破折号后的 more stimulating... 是补充评价，解释为什么他认为科幻小说值得读。

expressions.text: no more likely to be wrong

sentences.sentence: As his response, Wolpert commented, 'Science fiction has often been remarkable at predicting the future.
sentences.translation: 作为回应，沃尔珀特评论说：“科幻小说常常在预测未来方面表现出色。

structure.text: Science fiction has often been remarkable at predicting the future

difficult_points: As his response 表示“作为他的回答”，引出沃尔珀特对科幻问题的看法。
has often been 是现在完成时，强调这种表现从过去延续到现在的评价。
be remarkable at 后接动名词，表示“在……方面很突出”。

sentences.sentence: ... Science fiction has painted a vivid spectrum of possible futures, from cute and helpful robots to dystopian robotic societies.
sentences.translation: ……科幻小说描绘了一系列鲜明的可能未来，从可爱且有帮助的机器人，到反乌托邦式的机器人社会。

structure.text: a vivid spectrum of possible futures
from cute and helpful robots to dystopian robotic societies

difficult_points: has painted 是现在完成时，用 paint 比喻“描绘”。
a vivid spectrum of possible futures 表示范围很广的未来图景。
from... to... 说明这个 spectrum 的两端，从正面机器人到反乌托邦社会。

expressions.meaning_zh: 反乌托邦式机器人社会

sentences.sentence: Interestingly, almost no science fiction envisages a future without robots.'
sentences.translation: 有趣的是，几乎没有科幻小说会设想一个没有机器人的未来。”
difficult_points: Interestingly 是评价性副词，提示后面是沃尔珀特强调的观察。
almost no 表示“几乎没有”，是否定含义较强的限定词。
without robots 是介词短语，后置修饰 a future。

sentences.sentence: Finally, on the question of science fiction, Richardson pointed out that in modern society, people tend to think there is reality on the one hand, and fiction and fantasy on the other.
sentences.translation: 最后，关于科幻小说的问题，理查森指出，在现代社会中，人们往往认为一边是现实，另一边是小说和幻想。

structure.text: on the question of science fiction
that in modern society, people tend to think there is reality on the one hand, and fiction and fantasy on the other
there is reality on the one hand, and fiction and fantasy on the other

difficult_points: pointed out 后接 that 引导的宾语从句，说明理查森指出的现象。
people tend to think 后省略 that，引出人们通常的看法。
on the one hand... on the other 构成对照，表示现实和虚构被分成两边。

expressions.text: on the one hand, and on the other

sentences.sentence: She then explained that the division did not always exist, and that scientists and technologists made this separation because they wanted to carve out the sphere of their work.
sentences.translation: 她随后解释说，这种划分并非一直存在；科学家和技术专家作出这种分离，是因为他们想划定自己工作的领域。

structure.text: that the division did not always exist
and that scientists and technologists made this separation because they wanted to carve out the sphere of their work
because they wanted to carve out the sphere of their work

difficult_points: explained 后并列两个 that 从句，分别说明划分不存在于过去和划分产生的原因。
did not always exist 是否定 always，表示“并非一直存在”。
because 引导原因状语从句，说明科学家和技术专家为什么制造这种分离。

sentences.sentence: 'But the divide is not so clear cut, and that is why the worlds seem to collide at times,' she said.
sentences.translation: “但这种界限并没有那么泾渭分明，这就是为什么这些世界有时似乎会发生碰撞，”她说。

structure.text: and that is why the worlds seem to collide at times
why the worlds seem to collide at times

difficult_points: clear cut 是复合形容词，表示界限清楚、明确。
that is why... 中 that 指前半句“界限并不清楚”这一事实。
why 引导表语从句，解释这些世界有时碰撞的原因。
she said 是引述语，位于直接引语之后。

sentences.sentence: 'In some cases, we need to bring these different understandings together to get a whole perspective.
sentences.translation: “在某些情况下，我们需要把这些不同理解结合起来，以获得一个完整的视角。

structure.text: need to bring these different understandings together

difficult_points: In some cases 限定建议适用的情境，不表示所有情况都如此。
bring... together 表示把不同理解整合起来。
to get a whole perspective 是目的状语，说明整合不同理解的目的。

sentences.sentence: Perhaps then, we won't be so frightened that something we create as a copy of ourselves will be a [threat] to us.'
sentences.translation: 也许到那时，我们就不会那么害怕：我们创造出来作为自身复制品的东西会对我们构成威胁。

structure.text: that something we create as a copy of ourselves will be a [threat] to us
something we create as a copy of ourselves
we create as a copy of ourselves

difficult_points: Perhaps then 中 then 指在整合不同理解之后。
so frightened that... 是“如此害怕以至于／害怕……”结构，这里 that 引出恐惧的内容。
we create as a copy of ourselves 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 something。
方括号中的 threat 是编辑补足词，用来明确原文语义。

expressions.meaning_zh: 如此害怕以至于；很害怕……
作为我们自身的复制品
